baseline.notes: Run 43 broke the F1 ceiling at 0.409 (P 0.422, R 0.397, TP 62). Cross-game batch after Run 43 revealed the VLM coarse scan misses ~94% of events on sporting_ac vs ~50% on Rush — recognition fails with high confidence on windows that demonstrably contain events. Inspection of frames at actual 1280x720 VLM resolution showed pose cues ARE resolvable but ~1-2s throw-in/corner setup moments often fall BETWEEN the 3s-spaced frames. Run 44 = single-variable test: bump single_pass_frames 5->8 (~1.9s spacing) to directly attack the frame-sampling gap.

targets.notes: On Rush alone this run should at least hold Run 43's F1 (0.409). If frame sampling was a meaningful bottleneck even on Rush, we may see +0.01-0.02 F1 from recall pickup. A regression would suggest the denser frames confuse the VLM (more context drift, harder decisions). The real prize is cross-game: batch this config across the 4-game panel after confirming no Rush regression.

single_pass_frames.reason: ~60% more frames per window (~1.9s spacing vs 3s) to catch brief setup moments that were falling between samples on sporting_ac. Token budget OK: 8 frames at 1280x720 ~= 9.4K visual tokens + prompt < 16384 max_model_len.

notes: Run #44 (rerun) — single-variable frame-count test. First submission (job e865fa25, commit e60bd4f) was a NULL RUN: diagnostics showed n_frames=5 on all 685 windows and F1 was bit-identical to Run 43 (0.40924). Root cause: worker.py:366 getattr fallback was hardcoded to 5, overriding DualPassConfig's new default of 8. Commit 25f2b4d fixes the fallback. Worker restarted on 25f2b4d @ 22:37:19Z before this resubmission.
